title: Add logout/sign-out to clear current principal session on /admin

requirements.text: Add a visible “Sign out” control on the /admin page that logs the current Internet Identity principal out by calling the existing Internet Identity context clear/logout function, returning the user to the admin sign-in screen.
acceptanceCriteria: When an Internet Identity-authenticated user clicks “Sign out” on /admin, their session is cleared and the page returns to the “Admin Access” sign-in UI (the state where “Sign In with Internet Identity” is shown).
After signing out, admin-scoped React Query data is not shown for the previous identity, and admin status/leads queries re-run only after a new sign-in occurs.
User-facing button text is in English and clearly indicates signing out (e.g., “Sign out”).

requirements.text: When the user is authenticated via the credential-login mode on /admin, provide a “Sign out” control that clears only the credential-authenticated session state (username/password inputs, credential error/loading flags, and isCredentialAuthenticated) and returns the user to the admin sign-in options screen.
acceptanceCriteria: When a credential-authenticated user clicks “Sign out”, the UI returns to the sign-in options screen and no longer displays privileged/admin-only UI.
Credential state (username/password and error messages) is cleared after sign out.
Admin-scoped React Query data is invalidated/cleared so it does not remain visible after signing out.

constraints: Do not edit files in frontend immutable paths (including frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts); use its existing exported API from consumer components instead.
Keep all backend logic within the existing single-actor architecture; no new backend services.
nonGoals: Changing admin permissions or automatically granting admin to the current principal.
Implementing a backend-driven ‘force logout’ for other users.
Adding third-party authentication providers beyond Internet Identity.
